---
title: "Publications"
title-block-style: none
pagetitle: "Publications | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Searchable publications, working papers, book chapters and data outputs by Dr Mesfin Genie."
page-layout: full
image: /assets/img/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell"><p class="eyebrow">Publications</p><h1>Publications and research outputs</h1><p class="lead">A searchable record of journal articles, working papers, book chapters and data outputs. The list is generated from a single publication data file to avoid inconsistent counts across the website.</p></div></header>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><p><strong><span data-pub-count>44</span></strong> outputs shown.</p><div class="pub-toolbar" data-publication-toolbar aria-label="Publication search and filters"></div><p data-pub-empty hidden>No publications match the selected filters.</p><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2026</h2><span class="count-badge">15 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">An Open Repository of COVID-19 Vaccine Mandate Studies with a Worked Scoping Review of Quasi-experimental Evidence</a></h3><p class="authors">Fabio I. Martinenghi; Tushar P. Ojha; Anaïs Thomasson; Bianca Devsam; Sarah Drislane; Stavros Vasiliadis; Chiara Berardi; Aregawi Gebremariam; Arianna Dipalma; Teresa Gavaruzzi; Jeremy K. Ward; Francesco Paolucci; Margie Danchin; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Community First or My Body First? Moral Values and Vaccine Mandate Preferences</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Fabio I. Martinenghi; Francesco Paolucci; Jonathan Lake; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth; Margie Danchin; Teresa Gavaruzzi; Udo Evers</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Feeling Lonely? Preferences for Support Programmes to Reduce Loneliness among Older Adults in Australia: A Discrete Choice Experiment</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Allison W. Boyes; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Fit for Purpose? Assessing the Robustness of Discrete Choice Experiment Designs in the Context of Evolving Health Technologies</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Verity Watson; Michaël Schwarzinger; Stéphane Luchini</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.socscimed.2026.119463 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.70093" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Guidance or Misdirection? Unpacking the Role of Feedback in Health Preference Assessments</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Shelby D. Reed; Semra Ozdemir</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.70093" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1002/hec.70093 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Other applied economics" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Other applied economics</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s00181-026-02928-z" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Immigration and Preventive Care Utilization in Europe: Evidence from Retrospective Data</a></h3><p class="authors">Raluca Elena Buia; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Cristina Elisa Orso; Giacomo Pasini</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s00181-026-02928-z" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1007/s00181-026-02928-z ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Jabs and Lockdowns: Public Preferences for Vaccination Programmes in Chile</a></h3><p class="authors">Stefano Bruzzo-Gallardo; Josefa Henriquez; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong></p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101196 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Jabs or Societal Restrictions? Public Preferences for Vaccination Programmes in South Africa</a></h3><p class="authors">Aregawi G. Gebremariam; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Beidemaryam Wondwosen Admasu; D. Abegaz; J. G. Gatua; Surachat Ngorsuraches; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101195 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100450" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">No Jab, No Access? Preferences for Vaccine Mandates in Europe and Australia</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Fabio I. Martinenghi; Francesco Paolucci; Jonathan Lake; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth; Jessica Kaufman; Margie Danchin; Jeremy K. Ward; Teresa Gavaruzzi</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100450" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100450 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2025.10.011" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Priority for Self or Others? Incorporating Equity Considerations in a Preference-Based Health Value Assessment</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Surachat Ngorsuraches</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2025.10.011" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.jval.2025.10.011 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2025.101152" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Shots or Shutdowns? Public Preferences for COVID-19 Vaccines and Societal Restrictions in the US</a></h3><p class="authors">Ibrahim Alfayoumi; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Surachat Ngorsuraches</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2025.101152" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2025.101152 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s10198-025-01801-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Time Preferences and COVID-19 Vaccination Uptake</a></h3><p class="authors">Arthur E. Attema; Marcello Antonini; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Aleksandra Torbica; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s10198-025-01801-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1007/s10198-025-01801-7 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2025.101148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Understanding Vaccine Acceptance through Construal-Level Theory of Psychological Distance: Evidence from Singapore and South Korea</a></h3><p class="authors">Yevgen Bogodistov; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Ayman Fouda</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2025.101148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2025.101148 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Vaccination Policies and Hesitancy: Preferences, Trade-offs and Future Challenges</a></h3><p class="authors">Chiara Berardi; Ayman Fouda; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Aleksandra Torbica</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2026.101246 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2026" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.vaccine.2026.128825" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Vaccine Uptake in the Context of Mandate Announcement and Removal: Evidence from Europe and North America</a></h3><p class="authors">Aregawi G. Gebremariam; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Huong Le; Annette K. Regan; Frank H. Beard; Kristine Macartney; Francesco Paolucci; Hannah C. Moore; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.vaccine.2026.128825" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.vaccine.2026.128825 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2025</h2><span class="count-badge">7 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2025" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Book chapter"><div class="meta">Book chapter · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/B978-0-443-30168-1.00002-5" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Telehealth Evolution and Policy Response in Australia: Insights from the COVID-19 Pandemic</a></h3><p class="authors">Stefano Bruzzo-Gallardo; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; R. Gallagher; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/B978-0-443-30168-1.00002-5" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/B978-0-443-30168-1.00002-5 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2025" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2025.117687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Are We Ready for the Next Pandemic? Public Preferences and Trade-offs between Vaccine Characteristics and Societal Restrictions across 21 Countries</a></h3><p class="authors">Marcello Antonini; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Katie Attwell; Arthur E. Attema; Jeremy K. Ward; Alessia Melegaro; Aleksandra Torbica; Brian Kelly; Chiara Berardi; Ana Rita Sequeira; N. McGregor; A. Kellner; Shuli Brammli-Greenberg; Madeleine Hinwood; L. Murauskiene; Daiga Behmane; Zsolt J. Balogh; Terje P. Hagen; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2025.117687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.socscimed.2025.117687 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2025" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1215/03616878-12166725" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Democracy, Trust, and Political Orientation: Disentangling Mechanisms Shaping Individuals’ Vaccine Attitudes</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong></p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1215/03616878-12166725" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1215/03616878-12166725 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2025" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2024-097412" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Impact of Vaccine Mandates and Removals on COVID-19 Vaccine Uptake in Australia and International Comparators: A Study Protocol</a></h3><p class="authors">Aregawi G. Gebremariam; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Huong Le; Katie Attwell; Bette Liu; Annette K. Regan; Frank H. Beard; Kristine Macartney; Francesco Paolucci; Hannah C. Moore; Christopher C. Blyth</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2024-097412" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1136/bmjopen-2024-097412 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2025" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.2337/cd25-0047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Important Continuous Glucose Monitoring Attributes for Patients with Type 2 Diabetes</a></h3><p class="authors">Tim C. Lai; Heather P. Whitley; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Robert H. Eckel; Surachat Ngorsuraches</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.2337/cd25-0047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.2337/cd25-0047 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2025" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.euo.2024.06.010" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Patients’ Preferences for Cytoreductive Treatments in Newly Diagnosed Metastatic Prostate Cancer: The IP5-MATTER Study</a></h3><p class="authors">Martin J. Connor; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Tim Dudderidge; Hangjian Wu; Johanna Sukumar; Mark Beresford; Diletta Bianchini; C. Goh; G. Horan; P. Innominato; V. Khoo; N. Klimowska-Nassar; S. Madaan; S. Mangar; S. McCracken; P. Ostler; S. Paisey; A. Robinson; B. Rai; N. Sarwar; N. Srihari; K. T. Jayaprakash; M. Varughese; M. Winkler; Hashim U. Ahmed; Verity Watson</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.euo.2024.06.010" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.euo.2024.06.010 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2025" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Working paper"><div class="meta">Working paper · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.22714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Vaccinating Now or Vaccinating Later: Separating Pull-Forward and Net Effects Using a Dynamic Regression Discontinuity Design</a></h3><p class="authors">Fabio I. Martinenghi; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Katie Attwell; Huong Le; Hannah C. Moore; Aregawi G. Gebremariam; Bette Liu; Francesco Paolucci; Christopher C. Blyth</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2509.22714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.48550/arXiv.2509.22714 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2024</h2><span class="count-badge">7 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2024" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2024.07.001" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Choice Consistency in Discrete Choice Experiments: Does Numeracy Skill Matter?</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Nabin Poudel; Francesco Paolucci; Surachat Ngorsuraches</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2024.07.001" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.jval.2024.07.001 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2024" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ajpe.2024.101089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Doctor of Pharmacy Students’ Preferences for the Attributes of a Pharmacoeconomics Workshop: A Discrete Choice Experiment</a></h3><p class="authors">Surachat Ngorsuraches; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Anna Solomon</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ajpe.2024.101089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.ajpe.2024.101089 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2024" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2024.105082" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Political Determinants of COVID-19 Restrictions and Vaccine Rollouts: The Case of Regional Elections in Italy and Spain</a></h3><p class="authors">Pablo Arija Prieto; Marcello Antonini; Mehdi Ammi; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2024.105082" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.healthpol.2024.105082 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2024" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2024.100849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Public Preferences for Vaccination Campaigns in the COVID-19 Endemic Phase: Insights from the VaxPref Database</a></h3><p class="authors">Marcello Antonini; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Arthur E. Attema; Katie Attwell; Zsolt J. Balogh; Daiga Behmane; Chiara Berardi; Shuli Brammli-Greenberg; Andrew Greenland; Terje P. Hagen; Madeleine Hinwood; C. James; A. Kellner; Brian Kelly; L. Murauskiene; N. McGregor; Alessia Melegaro; N. Moy; Ana Rita Sequeira; R. Singh; Aleksandra Torbica; Jeremy K. Ward; D. Yang; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2024.100849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2024.100849 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2024" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2023.100792" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Vaccination and Non-pharmaceutical Interventions during COVID-19: Impact on Health and Non-health Outcomes in the US</a></h3><p class="authors">Asmita P. Khatiwada; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Aregawi G. Gebremariam; Tim C. Lai; Nabin Poudel; Surachat Ngorsuraches</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2023.100792" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2023.100792 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2024" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2023.100820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Vaccine Uptake and Effectiveness: Why Some African Countries Performed Better than Others?</a></h3><p class="authors">Aregawi G. Gebremariam; D. Abegaz; H. Y. Nigus; T. L. Argaw; M. Gerbaba; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2023.100820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2023.100820 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2024" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1302/0301-620X.106B12.BJJ-2023-1266.R2" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Willingness to Participate in Placebo-Controlled Surgical Trials of the Knee</a></h3><p class="authors">Laura Wall; Samantha Bunzli; Elizabeth Nelson; L. Hawke; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Madeleine Hinwood; Danielle Lang; M. Dowsey; P. Clarke; P. Choong; Zsolt J. Balogh; L. Lohmander; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1302/0301-620X.106B12.BJJ-2023-1266.R2" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1302/0301-620X.106B12.BJJ-2023-1266.R2 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2023</h2><span class="count-badge">1 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2023" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.4658" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Keeping an Eye on Cost: What Can Eye Tracking Tell Us about Attention to Cost Information in Discrete Choice Experiments?</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Mandy Ryan; Nicolas Krucien</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.4658" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1002/hec.4658 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2022</h2><span class="count-badge">4 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2022" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.euros.2021.10.003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">A Systematic Review of Patients’ Values, Preferences, and Expectations for the Treatment of Metastatic Prostate Cancer</a></h3><p class="authors">Martin J. Connor; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; David Burns; E. J. Bass; M. Gonzalez; N. Sarwar; A. Falconer; S. Mangar; T. Dudderidge; V. Khoo; M. Winkler; Hashim U. Ahmed; Verity Watson</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.euros.2021.10.003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.euros.2021.10.003 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2022" data-theme="Other applied economics" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Other applied economics</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.renene.2021.12.112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">How Do Renewable Energy and Urbanization Cause Carbon Emissions? Evidence from Advanced Panel Estimation Techniques</a></h3><p class="authors">Y. Sun; H. Li; Z. Andlib; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong></p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.renene.2021.12.112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.renene.2021.12.112 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2022" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2021-054155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Public Acceptability of Non-pharmaceutical Interventions to Control a Pandemic in the UK: A Discrete Choice Experiment</a></h3><p class="authors">Luis E. Loría-Rebolledo; Mandy Ryan; Verity Watson; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Ruben A. Sakowsky; Daniel Powell; Shantini Paranjothy</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2021-054155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1136/bmjopen-2021-054155 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2022" data-theme="Other applied economics" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Other applied economics</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/ieam.4500" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">The Role of Solar Energy and Eco-innovation in Reducing Environmental Degradation in China: Evidence from a QARDL Approach</a></h3><p class="authors">F. Chien; C.-C. Hsu; Z. Andlib; M. I. Shah; T. Ajaz; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong></p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/ieam.4500" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1002/ieam.4500 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2021</h2><span class="count-badge">4 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2021" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2021-048996" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Metastatic Prostate Cancer Men’s Attitudes towards Treatment of the Local Tumour and Metastasis Evaluative Research (IP5-MATTER): Protocol for a Prospective, Multicentre Discrete Choice Experiment Study</a></h3><p class="authors">Martin J. Connor; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Michael Gonzalez; N. Sarwar; K. T. Jayaprakash; G. Horan; F. Hosking-Jervis; N. Klimowska-Nassar; J. Sukumar; T. Pokrovska; D. Basak; A. Robinson; M. Beresford; B. Rai; S. Mangar; V. Khoo; T. Dudderidge; A. Falconer; M. Winkler; Verity Watson; Hashim U. Ahmed</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2021-048996" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1136/bmjopen-2021-048996 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2021" data-theme="Other applied economics" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Other applied economics</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s11356-021-13709-9" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">The Trade-off between Economic Growth and Environmental Quality: Does Economic Freedom Asymmetry Matter for Pakistan?</a></h3><p class="authors">Muhammad Tariq Majeed; Zhiyuan Yu; Adnan Maqbool; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; S. Ullah; Waheed Ahmad</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1007/s11356-021-13709-9" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1007/s11356-021-13709-9 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2021" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2021.113822" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">To Pay or Not to Pay? Cost Information Processing in the Valuation of Publicly Funded Healthcare</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Mandy Ryan; Nicolas Krucien</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socscimed.2021.113822" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.socscimed.2021.113822 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2021" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.4245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Weighting or Aggregating? Investigating Information Processing in Multi-attribute Choices</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Nicolas Krucien; Mandy Ryan</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.4245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1002/hec.4245 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2020</h2><span class="count-badge">3 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2020" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2020.08.019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">The COVID-19 Pandemic in Italy: Policy and Technology Impact on Health and Non-health Outcomes</a></h3><p class="authors">Chiara Berardi; Marcello Antonini; <strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; G. Cotugno; A. Lanteri; Adrian Melia; Francesco Paolucci</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.hlpt.2020.08.019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.hlpt.2020.08.019 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2020" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">The Role of Heterogeneity of Patients’ Preferences in Kidney Transplantation</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Antonio Nicolò; Giacomo Pasini</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1016/j.jhealeco.2020.102331 ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2020" data-theme="Public health policy" data-type="Journal article"><div class="meta">Journal article · Public health policy</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Understanding Public Preferences and Trade-offs for Government Responses during a Pandemic: A Protocol for a Discrete Choice Experiment in the UK</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Luis E. Loría-Rebolledo; Shantini Paranjothy; Daniel Powell; Mandy Ryan; Ruben A. Sakowsky; Verity Watson</p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.1136/bmjopen-2020-043477 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2019</h2><span class="count-badge">2 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2019" data-theme="Other applied economics" data-type="Data output"><div class="meta">Data output · Other applied economics</div><h3><a href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie#publications" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Combining the Retrospective Interviews of Wave 3 and Wave 7: The Third Release of the SHARE Job Episodes Panel</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong></p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie#publications" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Source ↗</a></p></article><article class="publication-record" data-year="2019" data-theme="Other applied economics" data-type="Data output"><div class="meta">Data output · Other applied economics</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.6103/SHARE.jep.800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SHARE Job Episodes Panel</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong></p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.6103/SHARE.jep.800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.6103/SHARE.jep.800 ↗</a></p></article></section><section data-year-group><div class="year-heading"><h2>2018</h2><span class="count-badge">1 outputs</span></div><article class="publication-record" data-year="2018" data-theme="Health preferences and choice" data-type="Working paper"><div class="meta">Working paper · Health preferences and choice</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.3270180" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Does Cognitive Ability Affect Choice Consistency?</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong></p><p class="citation"></p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.3270180" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI 10.2139/ssrn.3270180 ↗</a></p></article></section></div></section>
```
